titleStmt.title: SII 38.490: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 490. (A.R. No. 490 of 1912.) ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT. On the south wall, maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine, Siddharatnēśvara temple.

p.hi: King: Tribhuvanachakravartti Kullōttuṅgachōḷa III, who took Madurai and the crowned head of Pāṇḍya Year 21: 1199 A.D.

summary.p: Built in. Records that a brāhmaṇa priest of Śīkaṇṭa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam who owned kāṇi rights in the temple, misappropriated the jewels of the goddess (Periyanāchchiyār) and confessed his guilt. As he was declared culprit and śivadrōhi after an enquiry his land and slaves, were seized and given to the devotees (āṇḍārs), who were the palanquin bearers (śīpādantāndēvar), as kuḍinīṅga-dēvadānam. The temple accountant figures as the signatory.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrītribhuvanacakkaravattikaḷ śrī maturaiyum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭittalaiyum
[ko*ṇṭa]ruḷiya kulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu 2 10 1 t vaṭakarai Irājarājavaḷanāṭṭu vuṟṟa tū
yāṉa cikaṇṭaccaturvetimaṅkalattu pirāmaṇaṉ nālopāti kāṇi Uṭaiya koyi U
ṇināyanār koyilukku poṟpaṇṭāram ceytu varaccite Ivaṉ periyanācciyār tiruvuṭai
Eṭuttu koḷkaile Ivaṉai Ārāñca Iṭattu Ivaṉ tāne kaḷḷaṉāy civatturoyiyāy nicca
la Ivaṉno pātiyāl Uḷḷaṉa Eṭuppun ceyum natta maṉaiyum Aṭimaiyum Uṭaiyānāyi
nāṟkku tevatāṉamāy Aṉupokamāy varukaiyāle Ikkolo cipātāntavar Aṭamāṇṭi makaḷ tem
vilāḷ makaḷ maṇṭai kottu naṭuvaḷḷi makaḷ tevatāṇamRead tevatāṉam kottum taruviṉ makaḷ koviṉ kot
makaḷ kovāṇṭi kottu tevāṇṭi makaḷ Āṇṭamai kottu tevāṉṭi makaḷ Uyyavanti kottu
kottu naṅkai makaḷ tiruvīra �ttu Ivvaṉaivar vaḻiyil cipātantāṅki varukiṟa Āṇṭāṟkaḷu
Āticaṇṭecer Aruḷāl Iṉ cai pātiyum kuṭiniṉkā tevatāṉamaāka kalveṭṭiṉa(a)maikku Ā
koyil kaṇakku ṟicci Uṭai n tiṅkamāmaṇi Eḻuttu .
Digital edition of SII 38.490 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
